mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) over the prior three months signals robust consumer demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03 or positive returns occur in less than 50% of months with rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of observations or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high unemployment rate alongside a low federal funds rate indicates that monetary accommodation is insulating the economy from labor weakness, which compresses the equity risk premium and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A regression interaction term between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or the estimated interaction coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery periods, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals a rebound in manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery months, rising INDPRO coincides with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over a three-month window signals tightening labor markets and rising wage pressures that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE declines by more than 0.2pp over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirms a broad-based expansion in both consumption and manufacturing, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint acceleration of RSAFS and INDPRO does not produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of concurrent occurrences, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level implying year-over-year inflation above 3.0% signals erosion of real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-led expansions, accelerating housing starts (HOUST) predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because residential investment multiplies through durable goods demand and construction employment.
failure_condition: In months classified as housing-led expansions, rising HOUST coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.
